title: Problem 4.4 [?] — Odd Cycle Transversal in $P_5$-free graphs
statement_text: What is the computational complexity of the Odd Cycle Transversal problem in $P_5$-free graphs?
context_text: The paper explicitly states: 'The complexity status of Odd Cycle Transversal in $P_5$-free graphs remains open [?, Problem 4.4]: resolving this question was the original motivation of our work.' The problem is equivalent to Maximum Induced Bipartite Subgraph (by complementation), and is known to be NP-hard in $P_6$-free graphs.
paper_contribution: Provides a subexponential-time algorithm with running time $n^{O(\sqrt{n})}$ for Odd Cycle Transversal in $P_5$-free graphs, as a corollary of the main result (R3) on Max Partial $H$-Coloring in $P_5$-free graphs running in time $n^{O(\omega(G))}$.
notes: PDF source — the cite key for the reference containing Problem 4.4 was garbled to '[?]' during extraction; full complexity classification (P vs NP-hard) remains open after this paper.
paper_title: Finding large $H$-colorable subgraphs in hereditary graph classes
